macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Indian Equity Indices Close Strong with Nifty at 25,000
items.summary: Indian stock market ended on a strong note with Nifty reaching 25,000. All sectoral indices except FMCG and metals gained 1-3%, with realty, IT, auto, media, capital goods, consumer durables, telecom, and infrastructure leading the advance.

items.title: US Stock Market Rises Slightly Despite Middle East Tensions
items.summary: The three major US indices posted modest gains: Dow Jones up 0.1%, S&P 500 up 0.2%, and Nasdaq up 0.4%. Markets showed resilience to Middle East war concerns, with oil prices rising but financial markets demonstrating reduced sensitivity to geopolitical risks.

items.title: Federal Reserve Expected to Hold Rates Steady at 3.5%-3.75%
items.summary: The Federal Reserve is anticipated to maintain the federal funds rate at 3.5% to 3.75% at its March 18, 2026 meeting, holding steady amid high oil prices and lingering inflation despite some calls for rate cuts.

items.title: Oil Market Shows Substantial Upside as Rebalancing Begins
items.summary: Jeff Currie, chief strategy officer at Carlyle Energy Pathways, indicates the oil market faces a 'bumpy ride' with substantial upside potential as supply and demand rebalancing occurs, with physical oil prices already reaching $173 in some markets.

items.title: Tech Stocks Rally; Micron Hits Record High
items.summary: Large-cap technology stocks advanced with Alphabet up 1.7% and Amazon up 1.6%. Micron Technology surged 4.5% to reach an all-time high, benefiting from strength in the memory semiconductor sector.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Trades at $72,700 Amid Bullish Market Sentiment
items.summary: Bitcoin is trading at $72,700 with analysts viewing it as bullish and predicting a potential rally into April, while XRP stands at $1.48. Live trading sessions highlight Bitcoin for long positions and gold for shorts.[1][2]

items.title: Mastercard Acquires Stablecoin Startup BVNK
items.summary: Mastercard has acquired BVNK, a stablecoin startup, marking another Wall Street investment in crypto infrastructure. This follows announcements from major players like Citibank entering the space.[3]

items.title: Bitcoin Outperforms Assets During Geopolitical Tensions
items.summary: Bitcoin has risen around 13% this month and is acting as a safe haven amid war concerns, with surging flows over the past weeks. Intersections of crypto and AI are highlighted as an emerging trend.[3]

items.title: Market Reset Signals Point to Bitcoin Rally in Q2 2026
items.summary: Analysts identify a major reset signal predicting Bitcoin rally into April and longer-term growth over 12-24 months based on 18-year cycles, with BTC around $74,000 showing positive short-term outlook.[4]

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Niv-AI Emerges from Stealth with $12M Funding for GPU Optimization
items.summary: Niv-AI has secured $12 million in funding to develop sensors and data analytics that optimize GPU power usage in data centers, enhancing efficiency and computing capacity for advanced AI models.

items.title: Gurucul Report: AI as New Insider Threat in 90% of Organizations
items.summary: Gurucul's 2026 Insider Risk Report reveals that 90% of organizations face continuous insider incidents, with AI copilots and generative tools classified as insiders by 45%, driving the need for advanced machine learning-based risk management.

items.title: University of Minnesota AI Model Boosts Flood Forecasting Accuracy
items.summary: New knowledge-guided machine learning models from the University of Minnesota achieve higher accuracy in predicting floods than current physics-based methods, automating adjustments and aiding real-time forecasting.

items.title: Mistral Launches Forge Platform for Enterprise AI Model Training
items.summary: Mistral introduced Forge, a platform enabling enterprises to train custom AI models on proprietary data with reinforcement learning, already adopted by ASML, Ericsson, and the European Space Agency for specialized needs.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Ubisoft Appoints New Leaders for Creative House and Network
items.summary: Ubisoft announced leadership for its new Creative House model, with executives overseeing live titles like For Honor and casual franchises, alongside a general manager for the Creative Network from Massive Entertainment. This follows studio closures and further cost cuts amid Tencent's investment.

items.title: Tencent Reports Strong Q4 Profit Driven by Gaming Revenue Growth
items.summary: Tencent achieved double-digit net profit growth in Q4 2025, exceeding expectations, with domestic games revenue up 15% and international up 32% due to evergreen and new titles. The company is accelerating AI efforts and benefiting from Apple's reduced China commission.

items.title: Codex Mortis Announces March 26 Early Access Launch
items.summary: Crunchfest and Grolaf revealed that Codex Mortis, the world's first 100% AI-made necromantic survival bullet hell game, will launch in Early Access on March 26.

items.title: Moonton Games Unveils Film-Game IPs at Hong Kong FILMART
items.summary: Moonton Games hosted a film-game integration event at Hong Kong FILMART, unveiling three original film-game IPs to forge a new ecosystem.

items.title: Kennesaw State Student Wins Platinum at GDC for Game Narrative Review
items.summary: Kennesaw State University student Anna Green received the top Platinum Winner award in the Game Developers Conference's Game Narrative Review competition for her analysis paper.

items.title: ESA Launches iicon to Highlight Games in Cross-Industry Innovation
items.summary: The Entertainment Software Association announced the Interactive Innovation Conference (iicon) to showcase video games and game technologies' influence across industries.

items.title: Cloud Imperium Games Establishes Global HQ in Manchester, UK
items.summary: US-based Cloud Imperium Games selected Manchester as its global headquarters, joining other American firms expanding to the UK for talent and tax incentives.
